name: direct answer no preamble
response: Based on the system reminders, I can see the following MCP servers configured:  1. *Central Platform Metadata MCP* — platform metadata, ClickHouse queries, Grafana alerts, deployment data, app metrics 2. *Central Platform Tool MCP* — GCP logging/monitoring, Grafana, Jira, MongoDB, New Relic, Sentry, Tempo 3. *Azure DevOps* — repos, PRs, work items, branches, commits 4. *Figma* (`<http://claude.ai|claude.ai> Figma`) — design context, screenshots, Code Connect, FigJam 5. *Jira* (dedicated Jira MCP) — issues, sprints, boards, comments, workflows 6. *Sentry* — error tracking, issues, events, projects 7. *Sentry Fynd* — separate Sentry instance for Fynd platform 8. *Slack* (via Composio) — Slack tool schemas, connections, remote execution 9. *Filesystem* — local file operations, directory trees, media files  *9 MCP servers* total.  _sent by shadow.ai_

quality_scores.feedback: The response lists 9 servers but appears to conflate system reminders (which are Claude Code infrastructure) with actual MCP servers configured in shadow-ai; the bot should have queried the codebase to verify which servers are actually initialized and available.
